arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — RAG-Based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product documentation (PDFs) and get AI-generated answers grounded in that documentation. The app uses C# / Blazor for the UI, Postgres for storage, and a local file folder (`Docs/`) for PDF source files.

## Architecture: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

```
User Question
     │
     ▼
┌──────────┐    embed query    ┌───────────────────┐
│  Blazor  │ ───────────────►  │  OpenAI Embeddings │
│   Chat   │                   └────────┬──────────┘
│   Page   │                            │ vector
│          │◄──── streamed ────┐        ▼
└──────────┘     response     │  ┌──────────────┐
                               │  │  pgvector     │
                               │  │  (Postgres)   │
                               │  └──────┬───────┘
                               │         │ top-K chunks
                               │         ▼
                               │  ┌──────────────┐
                               └──│  OpenAI Chat  │
                                  │  (GPT-4o-mini)│
                                  └──────────────┘
```

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet (gpt-4o-mini) | Cost-effective, high quality for Q&A |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI + OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | Provider-agnostic embedding generation |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql | Postgres-native vectors; no extra infra |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` (`UglyToad.PdfPig`) | Free, pure-C#, no native dependencies |
| Chunking | Semantic paragraph-based chunking | Better retrieval relevance than fixed-size |
| UI framework | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming, single C# stack |
| Database | Postgres + EF Core (chat history) + pgvector (embeddings) | Single DB for both relational and vector data |

## NuGet Packages

```
Microsoft.Extensions.AI              (MEAI abstraction)
OpenAI                                (LLM + embedding provider)
Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions (vector store abstraction)
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL (EF Core + Postgres)
Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Npgsql (pgvector connector for MEVD)
UglyToad.PdfPig                       (PDF text extraction)
Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers               (client-side token counting)
```

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   ├── Chat.razor              # Main chat page
│   │   └── Chat.razor.cs           # Chat code-behind
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context
│   └── Migrations/                  # EF Core migrations
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat history entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session entity
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # MEVD vector record
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # Orchestrates RAG pipeline
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF → chunks → embeddings → pgvector
│   └── IDocumentSearchService.cs    # Vector search abstraction
├── Configuration/
│   └── AiOptions.cs                 # IOptions<T> for AI settings
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # Source documentation
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, pipeline
├── appsettings.json                 # Non-secret configuration
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project Setup & Package Installation
- Add all NuGet packages
- Configure .csproj for Blazor Server with Interactive SSR

### 2. Configuration & Options
- Create `AiOptions.cs` with model IDs, embedding dimensions, chunk size, relevance threshold
- Configure `appsettings.json` with non-secret settings
- API key loaded from environment variable / user-secrets (never committed)

### 3. Data Layer
- `ChatDbContext` with `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage` entities
- `DocumentChunk` as an MEVD `VectorStoreRecord` with embedding vector
- EF Core migrations for Postgres
- pgvector extension enabled via migration

### 4. Document Ingestion Service
- Read PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Split text into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens each)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into pgvector via MEVD
- Idempotent: skip re-ingestion of unchanged documents (hash-based)

### 5. Chat / RAG Service
- Accept user question
- Embed the question
- Search pgvector for top-K relevant chunks (K=5, min score 0.75)
- Build prompt with system message + retrieved chunks + user question
- Stream response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync`
- Include source attribution (chunk source + page number)
- Persist chat history to Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `App.razor` + `Routes.razor` + `MainLayout.razor` with layout/CSS
- `Chat.razor` page with:
  - Chat message list (scrollable)
  - Input textbox + send button
  - Streaming response display
  - Source citations shown per response
  - New session button

### 7. Program.cs Wiring
- Register EF Core with Postgres
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` with OpenAI provider
- Register pgvector vector store via MEVD
- Register `ChatService`, `DocumentIngestionService`
- Add Blazor Server services + interactive components
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- Add health checks

### 8. Guardrails (per skill guidance)
- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold (0.75) on vector search
- Source attribution in every response
- Token counting via Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Model version pinning (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18, text-embedding-3-small)

# HelpChatbot — RAG-Based Blazor Chatbot Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot that lets customers ask questions about internal product documentation (PDFs) and get AI-generated answers grounded in that documentation. The app uses C# / Blazor for the UI, Postgres for storage, and a local file folder (`Docs/`) for PDF source files.

## Architecture: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)

```
User Question
     │
     ▼
┌──────────┐    embed query    ┌───────────────────┐
│  Blazor  │ ───────────────►  │  OpenAI Embeddings │
│   Chat   │                   └────────┬──────────┘
│   Page   │                            │ vector
│          │◄──── streamed ────┐        ▼
└──────────┘     response     │  ┌──────────────┐
                               │  │  pgvector     │
                               │  │  (Postgres)   │
                               │  └──────┬───────┘
                               │         │ top-K chunks
                               │         ▼
                               │  ┌──────────────┐
                               └──│  OpenAI Chat  │
                                  │  (GPT-4o-mini)│
                                  └──────────────┘
```

## Technology Selection (per skill guidance)

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| LLM abstraction | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (`IChatClient`) | Single prompt→response with RAG context; no agentic loops needed |
| LLM provider | `OpenAI` NuGet (gpt-4o-mini) | Cost-effective, high quality for Q&A |
| Embeddings | `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI + OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | Provider-agnostic embedding generation |
| Vector store | `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions` + pgvector via Npgsql | Postgres-native vectors; no extra infra |
| PDF parsing | `PdfPig` (`UglyToad.PdfPig`) | Free, pure-C#, no native dependencies |
| Chunking | Semantic paragraph-based chunking | Better retrieval relevance than fixed-size |
| UI framework | Blazor Server (Interactive SSR) | Real-time streaming, single C# stack |
| Database | Postgres + EF Core (chat history) + pgvector (embeddings) | Single DB for both relational and vector data |

## NuGet Packages

```
Microsoft.Extensions.AI              (MEAI abstraction)
OpenAI                                (LLM + embedding provider)
Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions (vector store abstraction)
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL (EF Core + Postgres)
Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Npgsql (pgvector connector for MEVD)
UglyToad.PdfPig                       (PDF text extraction)
Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers               (client-side token counting)
```

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   ├── Chat.razor              # Main chat page
│   │   └── Chat.razor.cs           # Chat code-behind
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatDbContext.cs             # EF Core context
│   └── Migrations/                  # EF Core migrations
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs               # Chat history entity
│   ├── ChatSession.cs               # Session entity
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs             # MEVD vector record
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs               # Orchestrates RAG pipeline
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs  # PDF → chunks → embeddings → pgvector
│   └── IDocumentSearchService.cs    # Vector search abstraction
├── Configuration/
│   └── AiOptions.cs                 # IOptions<T> for AI settings
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf            # Source documentation
├── Program.cs                       # DI, middleware, pipeline
├── appsettings.json                 # Non-secret configuration
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project Setup & Package Installation
- Add all NuGet packages
- Configure .csproj for Blazor Server with Interactive SSR

### 2. Configuration & Options
- Create `AiOptions.cs` with model IDs, embedding dimensions, chunk size, relevance threshold
- Configure `appsettings.json` with non-secret settings
- API key loaded from environment variable / user-secrets (never committed)

### 3. Data Layer
- `ChatDbContext` with `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage` entities
- `DocumentChunk` as an MEVD `VectorStoreRecord` with embedding vector
- EF Core migrations for Postgres
- pgvector extension enabled via migration

### 4. Document Ingestion Service
- Read PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Split text into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens each)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into pgvector via MEVD
- Idempotent: skip re-ingestion of unchanged documents (hash-based)

### 5. Chat / RAG Service
- Accept user question
- Embed the question
- Search pgvector for top-K relevant chunks (K=5, min score 0.75)
- Build prompt with system message + retrieved chunks + user question
- Stream response via `IChatClient.GetStreamingResponseAsync`
- Include source attribution (chunk source + page number)
- Persist chat history to Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `App.razor` + `Routes.razor` + `MainLayout.razor` with layout/CSS
- `Chat.razor` page with:
  - Chat message list (scrollable)
  - Input textbox + send button
  - Streaming response display
  - Source citations shown per response
  - New session button

### 7. Program.cs Wiring
- Register EF Core with Postgres
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` with OpenAI provider
- Register pgvector vector store via MEVD
- Register `ChatService`, `DocumentIngestionService`
- Add Blazor Server services + interactive components
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- Add health checks

### 8. Guardrails (per skill guidance)
- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold (0.75) on vector search
- Source attribution in every response
- Token counting via Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Model version pinning (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18, text-embedding-3-small)

## Notes & Considerations

- **Postgres must have pgvector extension installed.** The migration will run `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector`.
- **API key security:** The OpenAI API key is read from `AI__ApiKey` env var or user-secrets. Never stored in appsettings.json.
- **Streaming:** Blazor Server supports real-time UI updates via SignalR — ideal for token-by-token LLM streaming.
- **Scalability:** For large doc sets, consider background ingestion via a hosted service rather than startup blocking.
- **Non-determinism:** LLM answers may vary. Temperature=0 + model pinning minimizes but doesn't eliminate this.
- **Cost control:** gpt-4o-mini + text-embedding-3-small keeps costs low; token logging enables monitoring.

- Relevance threshold (0.75) on vector search

- Model version pinning (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18, text-embedding-3-small)

  - New session button

- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` with OpenAI provider
- Register pgvector vector store via MEVD
- Register `ChatService`, `DocumentIngestionService`
- Add Blazor Server services + interactive components
- Run document ingestion on startup (or via admin endpoint)
- Add health checks

- Temperature = 0 for factual Q&A
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold (0.75) on vector search
- Source attribution in every response
- Token counting via Microsoft.ML.Tokenizers
- Model version pinning (gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18, text-embedding-3-small)

- **Streaming:** Blazor Server supports real-time UI updates via SignalR — ideal for token-by-token LLM streaming.
- **Scalability:** For large doc sets, consider background ingestion via a hosted service rather than startup blocking.
- **Non-determinism:** LLM answers may vary. Temperature=0 + model pinning minimizes but doesn't eliminate this.
- **Cost control:** gpt-4o-mini + text-embedding-3-small keeps costs low; token logging enables monitoring.

     │

└──────────┘     response     │  ┌──────────────┐

                               │  │  (Postgres)   │
                               │  └──────┬───────┘

**Key technology choices:**

| LLM | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` + OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` |

7. **Program.cs wiring** — DI registration, startup ingestion, health checks
8. **Guardrails** — temperature=0, model pinning, retry logic, token budgets
